exampleSentences: Anh và em đều thích đi du lịch vào mùa hè.
Cả nhà chị đều ăn sáng lúc bảy giờ sáng mỗi ngày.
Các bạn trong lớp đều muốn tham gia hoạt động thể thao chiều nay.
notes:
đều means all or both (for people or things in a group). It shows that every member of the group does the same thing or has the same quality.

It usually comes after the subject and before the verb or adjective.  

It often works with words like cả, hai, or plural nouns to stress “every one of them.”  
Don’t confuse it with mọi (every/each, used before a noun) — đều focuses on shared action or state, not counting.

translation.en: They all like to eat ice cream.

example.es: A todos les gusta comer pho.
example.vi: Mọi người đều thích ăn phở.

definitions.en: Indicates uniformity or equality; everyone or everything is included.
definitions.es: Indica uniformidad o igualdad; todos o todo está incluido.
definitions.vi: Chỉ sự đồng nhất hoặc bình đẳng; mọi người hoặc mọi thứ đều được bao gồm.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi ___ làm việc chăm chỉ.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Anh and em all like to travel in the summer.
Chị's whole family all eats breakfast at seven o'clock every morning.
The students in the class all want to join the sports activity this afternoon.
notes_md:
**đều** means *all* or *both* (for people or things in a group). It shows that every member of the group does the same thing or has the same quality.

It usually comes **after the subject** and before the verb or adjective.  

It often works with words like **cả**, **hai**, or plural nouns to stress “every one of them.”  
Don’t confuse it with **mọi** (every/each, used before a noun) — *đều* focuses on shared action or state, not counting.
